Do not edit this file directly. -->  # ARC-QUIC-CRT-0012 - QUIC Server-Role Certificate Continuation Architecture  ## Purpose  Describe the permanent server-role local Certificate continuation that lives behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam, extends the server floor after local ServerHello, Handshake key availability, and local EncryptedExtensions, formats one local leaf certificate into a single local Certificate message, and exposes outbound Certificate bytes through the existing library-owned handshake seam without claiming CertificateVerify, server Finished, or interop support.  ## Requirements Satisfied  - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0114  ## Design Summary  The connection library keeps server-role local Certificate emission as the next bridge-owned slice after the existing server floor rather than adding a second TLS stack. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello. Once that owner has accepted a supported ClientHello for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, the managed key-schedule owner has already published the local ServerHello and Handshake key availability, the local EncryptedExtensions continuation has been emitted from the local QUIC transport parameters, and the server-local leaf certificate seam carries a supported raw DER leaf certificate, the key schedule formats one deterministic local Certificate message with empty certificate_request_context and one zero-extension leaf entry, appends that message behind the local EncryptedExtensions at the transcript boundary, and exposes the local Certificate bytes as Handshake-level CryptoDataAvailable with the correct offset semantics on the existing handshake seam. The slice deliberately stops after local Certificate and does not synthesize CertificateVerify, server Finished, commit, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.  ## Key Components  - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsCipherSuiteProfile.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0114.cs  ## Risks  - The server-role certificate continuation must stay behind the existing bridge seam and must not grow into a broad TLS subsystem. - The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it. - Outbound Certificate availability must not be mistaken for CertificateVerify ownership, handshake completion, or interop support. - Server-role commit must remain blocked even though Handshake keys become available at the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary, local EncryptedExtensions is emitted on the existing handshake seam, and local Certificate is emitted on the existing handshake seam.  ## Related Code And Tests  -
